name: Q5214034
abstract: Dan "Slamfoot" Minor (August 10, 1909 – April 11, 1982) was an American jazz trombonist who featured in the bands of Count Basie, Cab Calloway and many others from the 1920s to the 1940s.

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: So they asked me would I consider to work with Rosetta Tharpe... So we made 'Two Little Fishes and Five Loaves of Bread' and it was a big hit.

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Durham came in, let's see now, Eddie Durham, it was either Dan Minor or Eddie Durham. I think it was Dan Minor when you........ It might have been Dan Minor. Yes, I think...... (Sure, it might have been Dan Minor?).

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Green
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] 1940 -- Ed Lewis, Al Killian, Harry Edison, Buck Clayton, Vic Dickenson, Dicky Wells, Dan Minor, Earl Warren, Tab Smith, Buddy Tate, Paul Bascomb. And, you know -- you've got Jo Jones and Walter Page there.

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Then Walter Page gave up his band and he joined the band along with Dan Minor and Buster Smith... Bennie Moten played one and three... when they wedded together

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Tate
about_quotes.evidence: The same, you know, all the guys you know, you know, Dan Minor and Lips Page was in the band, Joe Keyes and all them kind of cats.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Bert
about_quotes.evidence: And Dan Minor was a great guy too... I got to know Dickie later. And Dan Minor was a great guy too
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001
